Summary of what was found: Albea has a 2021-priority family, first published in 2023, on flexible cosmetic/personal-care tubes that incorporate PCR/post-consumer resin into a multilayer tube wall while keeping a virgin polyolefin outer layer for appearance and defect control.
Detailed patent mapping: Publication family: EP4166316A1; US20230112847A1; US12441519B2; family ID 78134862. Assignee: Albea Services SAS. Priority year: 2021. Filing year: 2021 EP / 2022 US. Publication year: 2023 EP and US application; 2025 US grant. Jurisdiction: EP / US. Legal status: EP pending; US active/granted. Component: tube skirt / side wall. Material route: multilayer polyolefin sheet with PCR, HDPE PCR options, optional EVOH/tie layers, and thin virgin outer layer. Claimed invention: flexible tube side wall formed from cast or calendered multilayer sheet with virgin polyolefin layer and PCR/recycled layer; PCR can be at least 30 wt% of multilayer and at least 20 wt% of total container. Problem solved: use recycled content without unacceptable outer-surface defects or loss of tube functionality. Cosmetic relevance: high for skincare, suncare, haircare, toothpaste, and squeezable tubes. Premium vs mass-market fit: stronger for mass-market and dermo/personal-care than ultra-premium clear/decorative tubes. Mapped topics: S19 primary; S14 secondary. IP risk signal: Moderate patent density / monitor claims; supplier-owned route; older-priority family with post-2022 publication. White-space note: high-PCR mono-material PE recyclability, premium decoration, and cap/head simplification may remain. Caution: included because of post-2022 publication/grant activity despite older priority.
Source name: Flexible tube container containing post-consumer resin / PCR flexible tube container — Google Patents — https://patents.google.com/patent/EP4166316A1 ; https://patents.google.com/patent/US20230112847A1/en

Summary of what was found: A related Albea family, granted in Europe in 2024, focuses on flexible tubes made from blown-film multilayer polymeric material and appears to act as a base platform for recyclable multilayer tube skirts and heads.

Problem solved: recycling-compatible flexible tube bodies with barrier and mechanical performance. Cosmetic relevance: high for creams, gels, lotions, dental, and tube-packed products. Source name: Flexible tube container — Google Patents — https://patents.google.com/patent/EP4124450B1/en

Summary of what was found: Albea filed a 2023-priority patent on an insert for a tube head using an HDPE-rich multilayer insert with polymeric barrier layers instead of conventional metal-containing barrier inserts.
Detailed patent mapping: Publication family: EP4556392A1; WO2025103862A1. Assignee: Albea Services SAS. Priority year: 2023. Filing year: 2023. Publication year: 2025. Jurisdiction: EP / WO. Legal status: pending. Component: tube head insert / neck seal area. Material route: multilayer insert with inner PE layer, outer PE layer, at least two polymeric barrier layers, and at least 50% HDPE; contrasted with conventional metal inserts. Claimed invention: colored HDPE-rich insert placed at shoulder/head, optionally sealing neck before first use, acting as barrier between product and tube head while improving detection and centering during assembly. Problem solved: barrier protection in head/neck zone without metal inserts, reducing oxidation and contamination risk. Cosmetic relevance: high for aggressive formulas, actives, peroxide, essential oils, sunscreen, oral care, and long shelf-life products. Premium vs mass-market fit: both, especially high-performance mass/premium products. Mapped topics: S18 primary; S14 secondary. IP risk signal: Recent active filings / higher caution; FTO review recommended around barrier head inserts. White-space note: co-molded barrier heads, mono-material barrier head architectures, or simplified opening systems may remain. Caution: enabling head/shoulder barrier technology more than direct sleeve lightweighting.
Source name: Insert for a tube head of a flexible tube container, associated tube head and flexible tube container — Google Patents — https://patents.google.com/patent/EP4556392A1/en
